title: Compute Job Ticket and Result
description: Contracts for the Drive-hub async compute hand-off (P60). A cloud surface CREATES a ticket file under Jobs/queue/ (create-only; cloud connectors cannot edit or move files). The local runner validates the ticket against this schema, executes ONLY an allowlisted job_type via the existing tool CLIs, and CREATES a result file under Jobs/results/. State transitions are always new files, never edits. Tickets are untrusted input: a ticket that fails validation is refused with a result of status 'refused', and no free-form instruction field is ever executed.

job_id.description: UUID minted by the submitter. Idempotency key: a job_id that already has a result/status file is never re-run.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp from the submitter.

origin.description: Which surface created the ticket. Informational; grants no authority.

requested_by.description: Free-text label of the requesting session or user role. Informational only; never parsed or executed.

job_type.description: Hard allowlist. The runner refuses anything else. Publishing, posting, sending, credential access, and shell passthrough are structurally absent from this list and MUST NOT be added without an approval-gated change (see tools/handoff/MAINTAINER_README.md).

params.description: Job-type-specific parameters. Validated per job_type by the runner before any subprocess; unknown keys are rejected. Values are passed as fixed argv items to the tool CLI, never shell-interpolated.

input_refs.description: Paths RELATIVE to the Drive hub root (for example 'Inbox/video1.mp4'). The runner resolves them against the hub root and refuses any path that escapes it (realpath containment, same rule as the wizard's folder confinement).

priority.description: Low-priority jobs run after normal ones in the same pass. No preemption.

consent_note.description: One line recording why this job was submitted (shown in the run log). Informational only.

status.description: 'refused' = the ticket failed validation or named a disallowed job_type/path (nothing ran). 'needs_input' = the tool returned a gap the human must resolve (null-and-flag, never guessed).

tool_version.description: The Creator OS VERSION string of the machine that ran the job.

outputs.description: Paths relative to the hub root, always under Jobs/results/ or Outbox/.

log_tail.description: Last lines of the tool's output for diagnosis. Truncated; never a full traceback dump to the user-facing summary.

human_review_required.description: Always true for any result a downstream action could follow. The runner computes and reports; humans decide.
